title: Protocol for the Generation and Statistical Validation of the SMARTCLOTH-Database for Dietary Control in Diabetes
author: María García-Rodríguez
materials: Install the required dependencies: pip install -r requirements.txt

steps.title: Verify that `models
steps.instruction: Verify that `models.json` and `generator_rules.json` are located in the `data/` directory.

steps.title: Create a Python virtual environment in the project root
steps.instruction: Create a Python virtual environment in the project root: python -m venv .venv

steps.title: Activate the virtual environment. On Linux/macOS
steps.instruction: Activate the virtual environment. On Linux/macOS: source .venv/bin/activate On Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate

steps.title: Confirm that `output
steps.instruction: Confirm that `output.json`, `1_models.xlsx`, `2_menus.xlsx`, `3_analysis.xlsx`, `frecuencies.xlsx`, and `validation_dataset.xlsx` are generated in the `outputs/` directory.

steps.title: In the terminal, clone the public code repository from GitHub
steps.instruction: In the terminal, clone the public code repository from GitHub. You must have Git installed on your computer git clone https://github.com/i22almoj/smartcloth-gendie.git

steps.title: Change to the root directory of the repository cd smartcloth-gendie
steps.instruction: Change to the root directory of the repository cd smartcloth-gendie

steps.title: Verify that each row contains the...
steps.instruction: Verify that each row contains the nutritional values for both models (BDCA/FCDB reference values and SMARTCLOTH values) for: - energy (kcal), - carbohydrates (g), - proteins (g), - lipids (g), together with the corresponding differences: - `Dif. Kcal`, - `Dif. Carb`, - `Dif. Prot`, - `Dif. Lip`.

steps.title: In JAMOVI, use the `Level` column...
steps.instruction: In JAMOVI, use the `Level` column to subset the dataset according to the aggregation level required for each analysis: - `FOOD` for individual food items, - `DISH` for composite dishes, - `GROUP` for food-group level summaries, - `DAY` for complete daily menus.

steps.title: In JAMOVI, assess the distribution of the differences using
steps.instruction: In JAMOVI, assess the distribution of the differences using: - Kolmogorov–Smirnov test with Lilliefors correction, - P–P plots, - Q–Q plots.

steps.title: In JAMOVI, calculate the intraclass correlation...
steps.instruction: In JAMOVI, calculate the intraclass correlation coefficient using the ICC(3,k) model with: - two-way mixed effects, - absolute agreement, - 95% confidence intervals.

steps.title: Perform the ICC analysis independently at...
steps.instruction: Perform the ICC analysis independently at the aggregation levels reported in the manuscript.

steps.title: Generate Bland–Altman plots to evaluate agreement between both methods
steps.instruction: Generate Bland–Altman plots to evaluate agreement between both methods.

steps.title: Export the statistical outputs and figures...
steps.instruction: Export the statistical outputs and figures to obtain the final validation results reported in the manuscript.

steps.title: Install JAMOVI Desktop (version 2
steps.instruction: Install JAMOVI Desktop (version 2.3.28 or a compatible version) on your computer. https://www.jamovi.org

description: Generate the summary tables reporting the nutritional differences between SMARTCLOTH and FCDB, including mean (SD) and range (minimum–maximum).
